active_projects.completion_summary: Added 31 new theorem examples to bimodal examples.py covering 76% of the BimodalLogic ProofChecker BX axiom system (31/42 axioms organized across 4 layers: propositional, S5 modal, BX temporal, and modal-temporal interaction). Discovered and documented two syntax bugs: TopOperator fails at runtime requiring use of explicit neg-bot expansion, and binary Until/Since operators require infix syntax. All 40 active bimodal tests pass (up from 12 before this task). BX7 linearity and MF_MODAL_FUTURE examples added but excluded from CI via KNOWN_TIMEOUT_EXAMPLES.

memory_candidates.content: In bimodal ModelChecker, TopOperator fails at runtime when used as argument to temporal operators. Use explicit neg-bot instead of top in temporal formula contexts.
Binary operators Until and Since require infix form: (event Until guard). The prefix form fails with KeyError.
BX modal_future axiom: Box(A) -> Box(Future(A)) is NOT a theorem under bimodal ModelChecker semantics (countermodel at N=1, M=2). The related Box(A) -> Future(Box(A)) IS valid.

active_projects.completion_summary: Added three frame constraint builder methods to BimodalSemantics (build_nullity_identity_constraint, build_converse_constraint, build_forward_comp_constraint) in semantic.py, integrated them into build_frame_constraints(), and created a dedicated test file with 8 passing unit tests. The implementation aligns with the Lean ProofChecker TaskFrame axioms and Compositional typeclass from Frame.lean. All 12 pre-existing bimodal tests continue to pass.

memory_candidates.content: Z3 quantifier performance tip: when implementing compositionality constraints with 5 quantified variables (w, v, u, d1, d2), use z3.MultiPattern(premise1, premise2) in z3.ForAll(..., patterns=[...]) to guide instantiation only when both premises are in the ground term set.

BimodalSemantics test fixture sizing: for unit tests that load the full frame_constraints list (including skolem_abundance_constraint), use N=2 (4 states) rather than N=3 (8 states) to prevent solver timeout.
